sections.paragraph:
f. (from the root רָבָה)-

(1) multitude, magnitude, 2Ch 9:6 30:18 .

(2) very great part, 1Ch 12:29.

(3) progeny, increase of a family, 1Sa 2:33.

(4) interest, usury, as if the increase of the principal, Lev 25:37 (comp. Gr. τόκος, from τίκτω; Lat. fenus, from feo, i.e. fero, pario; whence fetus, fecundus; see Gellius, xvi. 13). Arab. رِباً interest; IV. to take interest.
